requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'IceCache: Memory-efficient KV-cache Management for Long-Sequence LLMs' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: The models and datasets required for evaluation have been acquired.
Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model is available for inference.

verification.spec: Verify that meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct can be loaded via HuggingFace transformers AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained and tokenizer; confirm config.json shows hidden_size and num_attention_heads consistent with the 8B variant.

sub_tasks.requirements: Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 model is available for inference.

verification.spec: Verify that mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 can be loaded via HuggingFace transformers AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained and tokenizer; check config.json identifies the v0.2 variant.

sub_tasks.requirements: LongChat-7B-v1.5 model is available for inference.

verification.spec: Load LongChat-7B-v1.5 from HuggingFace (lmsys/longchat-7b-v1.5-32k) and verify the model and tokenizer instantiate successfully and can produce a forward pass on a short input.

sub_tasks.requirements: Qwen3-32B model is available for inference.

verification.spec: Verify that Qwen/Qwen3-32B can be loaded via HuggingFace transformers AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained; check config.json shows 32B-scale architecture.

sub_tasks.requirements: Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 model is available for inference.

verification.spec: Load Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 from HuggingFace and verify it instantiates and runs a short forward pass.

sub_tasks.requirements: The LongBench benchmark dataset (covering all 16 subtasks listed in Table 1) is available for evaluation.

verification.spec: Load the LongBench dataset and verify all 16 subtasks (NarrativeQA, Qasper, MultiFieldQA-en, HotpotQA, 2WikiMQA, Musique, GovReport, QMSum, MultiNews, TREC, TriviaQA, SAMSum, PassageCount, PassageRetrieval-en, LCC, RepoBench-P) are accessible with non-empty splits.

sub_tasks.requirements: The Passkey Retrieval dataset (Mohtashami & Jaggi, 2023) is available with context lengths from 10k to 100k words.

verification.spec: Generate or load Passkey Retrieval examples at context lengths spanning 10k to 100k words, with passkey insertions at positions from 0% to 95% in 5% increments, and verify each test case decodes into a prompt of approximately the requested word count.

sub_tasks.requirements: The GSM8K dataset is available for Chain-of-Thought reasoning evaluation.

verification.spec: Load the GSM8K dataset and verify the test split is accessible with the expected schema (question, answer with numeric ground-truth).

sub_tasks.requirements: The RULER benchmark dataset (Single-NIAH, Multi-keys NIAH, QA tasks) is available at context lengths 150k, 200k, and 250k tokens.

verification.spec: Load/generate RULER benchmark examples for Single-NIAH, Multi-keys NIAH, and QA tasks at context lengths of 150k, 200k, and 250k tokens, and verify token counts using the Qwen3 tokenizer.

sub_tasks.requirements: The LongGenBench dataset is available for long-form generation evaluation.

verification.spec: Load the LongGenBench dataset and verify the long-form generation examples are accessible.

sub_tasks.requirements: The environment and infrastructure required to run IceCache experiments have been set up.
An NVIDIA A100 40GB PCIe GPU is available for experiments with small models.

verification.spec: Run nvidia-smi and torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0); assert device name contains 'A100' and total memory is approximately 40GB (>=39GB and <=41GB).

sub_tasks.requirements: An NVIDIA H100 80GB PCIe GPU is available for experiments with large models.

verification.spec: Run nvidia-smi and torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0); assert device name contains 'H100' and total memory is approximately 80GB (>=79GB).

sub_tasks.requirements: The number of CPU threads is fixed to 64 for all experiments.

verification.spec: Inspect the experiment configuration / launch script and confirm that the CPU thread count (torch.set_num_threads, OMP_NUM_THREADS, or analogous DCI thread parameter) is set to exactly 64.

sub_tasks.requirements: CUDA 12.2, PyTorch 2.4.1, and HuggingFace Transformers 4.57.1 are installed.

verification.spec: Check torch.version.cuda == '12.2', torch.__version__ starts with '2.4.1', and transformers.__version__ == '4.57.1'.

sub_tasks.requirements: FlashInfer is used as the attention kernel backend for the implementation.

verification.spec: Import flashinfer module; verify the attention kernel invocation in the IceCache codebase routes through flashinfer (e.g., flashinfer.prefill or flashinfer.decode functions are referenced in the attention layer).

sub_tasks.requirements: The Passkey Retrieval evaluation (Section 5.2) has been reproduced.
For each context length, 100 test cases are generated with passkeys inserted at positions 0% to 95% in 5% increments.

verification.spec: Inspect the passkey retrieval evaluation script/config and verify that for each tested context length it generates exactly 100 test cases, with passkey insertion positions sweeping from 0% to 95% of the context length in 5% increments (i.e., 20 distinct positions: 0,5,10,...,95). Verify by importing the test-case generator and checking len(cases)==100 per length and that the set of insertion-position percentages equals {0,5,...,95}.

sub_tasks.requirements: IceCache is evaluated at cache budgets of 256, 128, and 64 tokens on the Passkey Retrieval task.

verification.spec: Run the IceCache passkey retrieval evaluation pipeline on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with cache budget configured to each of {256, 128, 64} tokens. Confirm the pipeline accepts and runs to completion under all three budget settings, producing per-budget accuracy outputs (as shown in Figure 6 panels a/b/c). The check passes if the evaluation can be invoked with --cache_budget 256, 128, and 64 and emits results for each.

sub_tasks.requirements: IceCache achieves 100% retrieval accuracy on Passkey Retrieval across all three cache budgets (256, 128, 64) on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct.

verification.spec: Run Passkey Retrieval evaluation (Mohtashami & Jaggi 2023) using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with IceCache at each budget in {64,128,256}. For each cache budget, evaluate 100 cases at context lengths 10k..100k words with passkey positions 0%..95% in 5% increments. Compute retrieval accuracy (% exact-match of passkey) and assert it is 100% for all three budgets.

sub_tasks.requirements: The latency analysis (Section 5.3.2) on a 36k-token sequence has been reproduced.
IceCache achieves TT2T of ~7.7 seconds on a 36k-token sequence (Figure 7a).

verification.spec: On an A100 40GB PCIe with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, prefill a 36k-token sequence and measure the wall-clock time from prefill start until the second generated token is produced (TT2T). Repeat over 5 runs and take the mean; assert mean TT2T equals 7.7 seconds within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: IceCache(reuse) achieves TT2T of ~5.9 seconds on a 36k-token sequence.

verification.spec: Measure TT2T for IceCache(reuse) on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with 36k-token prompt on A100 (PCIe), verify ~5.9 seconds.

sub_tasks.requirements: IceCache(reuse) achieves decoding time of ~0.06 seconds per token on a 36k-token sequence.

verification.spec: Measure average time per generated token for IceCache(reuse) on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with 36k input on A100, generating >=100 tokens; verify ~0.06s.

sub_tasks.requirements: IceCache achieves accuracy percentage of ~99.0% relative to full KV-cache while maintaining competitive decoding latency.

verification.spec: Compute IceCache LongBench average accuracy on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct (budget=256) divided by Full KV average accuracy on same setting; verify ratio is ~99% (i.e., 49.0/49.5).

sub_tasks.requirements: The GSM8K Chain-of-Thought reasoning evaluation (Section 5.4) has been reproduced.
GSM8K is evaluated with Chain-of-Thought prompting on Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 using a 10% budget.

verification.spec: Inspect GSM8K evaluation script/config: verify Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 is loaded, the prompting format includes chain-of-thought exemplars (8-shot CoT standard), and the cache budget for compared methods is set to 10% of input length.

sub_tasks.requirements: Full KV achieves ~48.2% accuracy on GSM8K CoT with Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.

verification.spec: Run full KV-cache inference with Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 on GSM8K test set using CoT prompting; verify final answer exact-match accuracy ~48.2%.

sub_tasks.requirements: IceCache at 10% budget achieves ~47.4% accuracy on GSM8K CoT (best among compared budget-constrained methods).

verification.spec: Run IceCache on Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 over the GSM8K test set with Chain-of-Thought prompting using a 10% KV-cache budget. Compute exact-match accuracy on the numeric answer and compare to the reported 47.4%.

sub_tasks.requirements: PQCache at 10% budget achieves ~46.2% accuracy on GSM8K CoT.

verification.spec: Run PQCache with 10% budget on Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 over GSM8K CoT, verify accuracy ~46.2%.

sub_tasks.requirements: The LongGenBench evaluation (Appendix C) has been reproduced.
LongGenBench is evaluated using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with a 256-token budget for IceCache, PQCache, and Full KV.

verification.spec: Inspect the LongGenBench evaluation script and confirm that it loads Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct and runs IceCache, PQCache, and Full KV configurations using a KV-cache budget of 256 tokens.

sub_tasks.requirements: Full KV achieves Completion Rate ~97.627, Accuracy Once ~0.349, Accuracy Range ~0.488, Accuracy Periodic ~0.135, Avg. Accuracy ~0.324 on LongGenBench.

verification.spec: Run Full KV-cache Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct on LongGenBench and verify Completion Rate ~97.627, Accuracy Once ~0.349, Accuracy Range ~0.488, Accuracy Periodic ~0.135, Avg. Accuracy ~0.324.

sub_tasks.requirements: IceCache achieves Completion Rate ~95.322, Avg. Accuracy ~0.331 on LongGenBench, substantially outperforming PQCache (88.691 / 0.273).

verification.spec: Run IceCache (budget 256) on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct over LongGenBench; verify Completion Rate ~95.322 and Avg. Accuracy ~0.331, both higher than PQCache's reported (88.691 / 0.273).

sub_tasks.requirements: Logging and analysis infrastructure for reporting results has been implemented.
Per-task and average LongBench score logging across the 16 subtasks has been implemented.

verification.spec: Run the LongBench evaluation script on a small subset and verify that per-task scores for all 16 LongBench subtasks (NrtvQA, Qasper, MF-en, HotpotQA, 2WikiMQA, Musique, GovReport, QMSum, MultiNews, TREC, TriviaQA, SAMSum, PCount, PRe, Lcc, RB-P) and an average score are emitted to the log/output file.

sub_tasks.requirements: Latency measurement utilities for TT2T, TPOT, and per-token decode latency have been implemented and logged.

verification.spec: Run a short decoding job and verify that the latency utility logs (a) Time-To-Second-Token (TT2T) in seconds, (b) Time-Per-Output-Token (TPOT) in ms, and (c) average per-token decode latency. Confirm reasonable non-zero positive numbers in the produced output.

sub_tasks.requirements: CUDA memory footprint measurement and logging has been implemented for the trade-off plot in Figure 1.

verification.spec: Run a short inference job and verify that the script measures and logs peak CUDA memory footprint (in GB) using torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated or equivalent, producing the values used for Figure 1's trade-off plot.

sub_tasks.requirements: Passkey retrieval accuracy heatmap (by context length and insertion position) is produced as in Figure 6.

verification.spec: Run the passkey retrieval evaluation across context lengths (e.g., 10k-100k words) and insertion positions (0-95% in 5% steps), and verify that a 2D heatmap image (insertion position vs. context length) is produced with retrieval accuracy values, replicating the structure of Figure 6.

sub_tasks.requirements: Additional coverage added by critique pass
Numerical claims from paper tables/figures

Code has been implemented and run such that Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with the full KV-cache (no offloading, no compression) reproduces LongBench per-task scores: NrtvQA=30.2, Qasper=45.5, MF-en=54.9, HotpotQA=55.5, 2WikiMQA=46.7, Musique=31.3, GovReport=35.2, QMSum=25.2, MultiNews=27.2, TREC=72.5, TriviaQA=91.7, SAMSum=43.8, PCount=8.4, PRe=99.5, Lcc=65.1, RB-P=58.8 (average 49.5).

verification.spec: Run the Full-KV baseline pipeline on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct over all 16 LongBench tasks; compute task-level scores using LongBench metrics; assert that the LongBench average is within tolerance of 49.5.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct using ground-truth top-k KV-cache (oracle selection with budget 256) reproduces a LongBench average of 49.8.

verification.spec: Implement oracle top-k KV-cache selection: at each decoding step, select the 256 keys with the largest exact attention scores; run on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct across LongBench; assert LongBench average is within tolerance of 49.8.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with a 256-token KV-cache budget reproduces LongBench per-task scores including NrtvQA=30.6, Qasper=44.7, MF-en=56.3, HotpotQA=55.2, PRe=100.0, Lcc=62.5, RB-P=56.4 (overall average 49.0).

verification.spec: Run the full IceCache pipeline on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with KV budget=256 across all 16 LongBench tasks; compute task scores; assert LongBench average is within tolerance of 49.0.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with a 64-token KV-cache budget reproduces LongBench scores including NrtvQA=27.4, MF-en=55.7, PRe=99.5, average 47.8.

verification.spec: Run IceCache on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with KV budget=64 across all 16 LongBench tasks; assert LongBench average is within tolerance of 47.8.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 with the full KV-cache reproduces LongBench per-task scores: NrtvQA=26.8, Qasper=33.1, MF-en=49.3, HotpotQA=42.8, 2WikiMQA=27.3, Musique=18.8, GovReport=33.0, QMSum=24.2, MultiNews=27.1, TREC=71.0, TriviaQA=86.2, SAMSum=42.8, PCount=2.9, PRe=87.0, Lcc=56.9, RB-P=54.3 (average 42.2).

verification.spec: Run Full-KV baseline on Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 across all 16 LongBench tasks; assert LongBench average is within tolerance of 42.2.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 with ground-truth top-k KV-cache (budget=256) reproduces a LongBench average of 42.6.

verification.spec: Implement oracle top-k KV selection (budget=256) for Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2; run LongBench; assert average within tolerance of 42.6.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache on Qwen3-32B with a 256-token KV-cache budget reproduces per-task LongBench scores including NrtvQA=32.2, Qasper=44.1, PRe=100.0, with overall average 43.1.

verification.spec: Run IceCache on Qwen3-32B with KV budget=256 on LongBench; assert LongBench average within tolerance of 43.1.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache on Qwen3-32B with a 128-token KV-cache budget achieves a LongBench average of 42.6.

verification.spec: Run IceCache on Qwen3-32B with KV budget=128 on LongBench; assert LongBench average within tolerance of 42.6.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache on LongChat-7B-v1.5 with a 128-token KV-cache budget achieves a LongBench average of 34.3.

verification.spec: Run IceCache on LongChat-7B-v1.5 with KV budget=128 across all 16 LongBench tasks; assert LongBench average within tolerance of 34.3.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache with a 256-token KV-cache budget maintains at least 99% of the LongBench accuracy of the full KV-cache model (e.g. on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, 49.0 / 49.5 ≈ 99%).

verification.spec: Compute LongBench averages for IceCache (budget=256) and Full-KV on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct; compute ratio ice_avg / full_avg; assert ratio >= 0.99 within tolerance.
verification.paper_cite: Abstract: 'maintains 99% of the original accuracy ... with a 256-token budget'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache attains LongBench accuracy competitive with or superior to other offloading-based methods (OmniKV, PQCache, ArkVale, MagicPig) while using only 25% of their KV-cache token budget (e.g. IceCache@64 vs baselines@256 on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct: 47.8 vs best baseline 47.3).

verification.spec: On Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, run IceCache with budget=64 and the strongest offloading baseline (PQCache) with budget=256 on LongBench; assert IceCache@64 average >= PQCache@256 average within tolerance (paper: 47.8 >= 47.3).
verification.paper_cite: Abstract: 'competitive or even superior latency and accuracy while using only 25% of the KV-cache token budget'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache achieves 100% passkey retrieval accuracy on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct across cache budgets of 256, 128, and 64, for context lengths spanning 10k to 100k words with passkeys inserted at relative positions 0%-95% in 5% increments

verification.spec: Run passkey retrieval benchmark using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with IceCache at each of {256,128,64} token budgets; for each context length in {10k,30k,60k,100k} words, generate 100 test cases with passkeys at positions 0%-95% step 5%; compute retrieval accuracy; assert accuracy == 1.0 (within tolerance) for all configurations

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Time-To-Second-Token (TT2T) at 36k sequence length on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct matches reported values: SnapKV=0.55s, StreamingLLM=0.13s, OmniKV=5.8s, ArkVale=7.4s, PQCache=13.3s, IceCache=7.7s, IceCache(reuse)=5.9s

verification.spec: On A100 40GB, run Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with a 36k-token input under each method (SnapKV, StreamingLLM, OmniKV, ArkVale, PQCache, IceCache, IceCache-reuse); measure wall-clock time from after first token generation to second token completion; assert each measured TT2T matches paper value within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Time-Per-Output-Token (TPOT) at 36k sequence length on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct matches reported values: SnapKV=0.03s, StreamingLLM=0.03s, OmniKV=0.05s, PQCache=0.13s, IceCache(reuse)=0.06s

verification.spec: On A100 40GB, run Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct decoding with 36k-token prompt and generate at least 32 output tokens per method; compute average seconds-per-token; assert measured TPOT for IceCache(reuse) ~= 0.06s within tolerance, and other methods match paper values

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Figure 1's CUDA memory footprint and TPOT for IceCache vs OmniKV, PQCache, and Full Attention (with/without offloading) on A100 at 36k sequence length are reproduced, demonstrating IceCache's best memory-latency trade-off

verification.spec: On A100 40GB, measure peak CUDA memory (via torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated) and TPOT for IceCache, OmniKV, PQCache, Full Attention w/o offloading, and Full Attention w/ offloading at 36k input length on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct; assert IceCache has lowest TPOT among methods with low memory and shows Pareto-optimal trade-off as in Figure 1

sub_tasks.paper_unit: k token sequence length test point

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on RULER benchmark at 200k token context with Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507, Full KV achieves 92.0 on Multi-keys NIAH and 95.0 on QA

verification.spec: Run RULER benchmark with 200k-token context using Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 (full KV-cache) on H100 80GB; compute accuracy on Multi-keys NIAH and QA subtasks; assert Multi-keys NIAH ~= 92.0 and QA ~= 95.0

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on RULER benchmark at 250k token context with Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507, IceCache(reuse) achieves 92.0 on Multi-keys NIAH and 92.0 on QA with a 256-token budget

verification.spec: Run RULER benchmark with 250k-token context using Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 and IceCache(reuse) with 256-token budget on H100 80GB; compute accuracy on Multi-keys NIAH and QA subtasks; assert Multi-keys NIAH ~= 92.0 and QA ~= 92.0

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that per-token decode latency at 300k context length on Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 follows the scaling shown in Figure 8, with Full KV growing sharply while IceCache and IceCache(reuse) grow significantly slower

verification.spec: On H100 80GB with Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 and 64 CPU threads, measure decode latency per token at 300k context length for Full KV, IceCache, and IceCache(reuse); assert IceCache and IceCache(r) latency < Full KV latency, with values consistent with Figure 8 trend

sub_tasks.paper_unit: k token context length test point

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on LongBench with a 256-token budget on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, IceCache maintains at least 99% of the average accuracy of the full KV-cache model

verification.spec: Run LongBench across all 16 subtasks using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with Full KV-cache and with IceCache at 256-token budget; compute average accuracy; assert (IceCache_avg / Full_avg) >= 0.99 (paper reports 49.0/49.5 = 99.0%)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache attains accuracy competitive with or superior to offloading-based baselines (OmniKV, PQCache, ArkVale, MagicPig) while using only 25% of their KV-cache token budget on LongBench

verification.spec: Compare LongBench average accuracy of IceCache at 64-token budget against PQCache/OmniKV/ArkVale/MagicPig at 256-token budget on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct; assert IceCache_64 (47.8) >= max(baseline_256) within tolerance, demonstrating 25% budget competitiveness

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on GSM8K with Chain-of-Thought prompting using Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 at a 10% KV-cache budget, IceCache achieves 47.4% accuracy, outperforming PQCache (46.2%) and approaching Full KV-cache (48.2%)

verification.spec: Run GSM8K with 8-shot CoT prompting on Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 using IceCache at 10% budget; compute exact-match accuracy on test set; assert accuracy ~= 47.4% within tolerance and exceeds PQCache baseline at same budget

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on LongBench with Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 at 256-token budget, IceCache achieves average accuracy of 41.7, leading the best baseline (MagicPig 39.1) by 2.6 points

verification.spec: Run LongBench all 16 subtasks on Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 with IceCache at 256-token budget; compute average score; assert avg ~= 41.7 within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on LongBench with Qwen3-32B at 64-token budget, IceCache retains 97.2% of full KV-cache average accuracy (42.2 vs 43.4); and at 256-token budget retains 99.3% (43.1 vs 43.4)

verification.spec: Run LongBench on Qwen3-32B with Full KV and IceCache at budgets {64,256} on H100 80GB; compute average accuracy; assert IceCache_64/Full >= 0.972 and IceCache_256/Full >= 0.993 within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on LongBench with LongChat-7B-v1.5 (standard multi-head attention) at 64-token budget, IceCache retains 96.3% of full KV-cache accuracy (33.9 vs 35.2); and at 256-token budget retains 99.4% (35.0 vs 35.2)

verification.spec: Run LongBench on LongChat-7B-v1.5 with Full KV and IceCache at budgets {64,256}; compute average accuracy; assert IceCache_64/Full >= 0.963 and IceCache_256/Full >= 0.994 within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that TPOT latency breakdown at 36k sequence on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct totals approximately 0.11s, with DCI-query module contributing ~0.05s, decoding ~0.04s, GPU-CPU offloading ~0.015s, and other miscellaneous ~0.005s

verification.spec: On A100 40GB, profile IceCache decoding at 36k sequence length on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct using CUDA events / cProfile; record time spent in DCI-query, decoding, offloading (loading), and other; assert total ~= 0.11s and DCI-query ~= 0.05s within tolerance

verification.spec: Run passkey retrieval benchmark on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with IceCache at budget=256 over context lengths 10k-100k words and insertion positions 0%-95% in 5% increments (100 cases per length); assert measured retrieval accuracy == 100% within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache achieves 100% passkey retrieval accuracy on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct at cache budget 128 across positions 0%-95% of context length

verification.spec: Run passkey retrieval benchmark on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with IceCache at budget=128 over context lengths 10k-100k words; assert measured retrieval accuracy == 100% within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache achieves 100% passkey retrieval accuracy on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct at cache budget 64 across positions 0%-95% of context length

verification.spec: Run passkey retrieval benchmark on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with IceCache at budget=64 over context lengths 10k-100k words; assert measured retrieval accuracy == 100% within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache on Qwen3-32B with budget 64 retains 97.2% of full KV-cache average accuracy on LongBench (42.2 vs 43.4)

verification.spec: Run LongBench evaluation on Qwen3-32B with IceCache budget=64; compute ratio of average accuracy to full KV-cache baseline (43.4); assert ratio == 0.972 within tolerance

verification.spec: Run LongBench evaluation on Qwen3-32B with IceCache budget=256; compute ratio of average accuracy to full KV-cache baseline (43.4); assert ratio == 0.993 within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache on LongChat-7B-v1.5 with budget 64 retains 96.3% of full KV-cache average accuracy on LongBench (33.9 vs 35.2)

verification.spec: Run LongBench evaluation on LongChat-7B-v1.5 with IceCache budget=64; compute ratio of average accuracy to full KV-cache baseline (35.2); assert ratio == 0.963 within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that an ablation comparing IceCache applied to all layers vs IceCache skipping the first two layers is performed, showing that skipping the first two layers (the paper's default) preserves higher accuracy than applying to all layers

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that an ablation over the promotion ratio r (e.g., r in {0.1, 0.25, 0.5}) of the M-DCI tree is performed and accuracy/latency are reported

verification.spec: Run IceCache on a short LongBench subset with at least three promotion ratios r; collect (accuracy, decode-latency) pairs; assert that the sweep ran end-to-end and that latency varies monotonically with r in the expected direction (smaller r => fewer top-level points => faster query)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that an ablation over page size (e.g., 8, 16, 32) is performed, measuring its effect on retrieval hit rate and downstream accuracy under a fixed token budget

verification.spec: Run IceCache on a passkey retrieval or LongBench subtask with at least three page sizes at a fixed budget of 256 tokens; record retrieval recall of significant keys and accuracy per setting; assert all runs complete and produce numeric metrics

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that an ablation comparing head-specific DCI-trees (one tree per attention head) vs a single shared DCI-tree across heads demonstrates that head-specific trees yield higher accuracy

verification.spec: Run IceCache on a LongBench subtask (e.g., 2WikiMQA) at budget=256 with (a) one DCI-tree per attention head (default) and (b) a single shared DCI-tree; assert configuration (a) achieves higher accuracy than (b)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that an ablation comparing GQA per-head page selection vs GQA group-union page sharing (union of selected pages across heads within a group) is performed, with the union scheme reported as the default following Yuan et al. 2025

verification.spec: Run IceCache on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct on a LongBench subtask under (a) GQA page-union sharing (default) and (b) per-head independent page selection; assert both runs complete and report accuracy and effective unique pages loaded per group

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented such that during the prefill phase, KV embeddings for each transformer layer are computed on GPU and then offloaded to CPU memory (per-layer), as described in Algorithm 1 / Section 4.5

verification.spec: Run prefill on a 4k-token input through a Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model with IceCache enabled; after prefill, assert that for each non-skipped layer the KV cache resides in pinned CPU memory (e.g., tensor.device.type=='cpu' and is_pinned()) and that GPU resident KV memory is bounded by sink+window allocation

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that during the decoding phase, for each generated query token, PAGE-SELECT (Algorithm 3) is invoked to retrieve the top-k relevant pages from the DCI-tree per attention head

verification.spec: Instrument PAGE-SELECT with a counter; run decoding of 16 tokens on a 4k-token prompt; assert PAGE-SELECT was invoked at least once per decoding step per layer (excluding skipped layers) and that the returned page set size is bounded by the configured budget

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that during decoding, the newly generated token's key embedding is inserted into the DCI-tree using the random promotion scheme with promotion ratio r, and assigned a parent at the next higher level via 1-NN query

verification.spec: Run decoding for 100 tokens; track the total number of nodes inserted at each level of the DCI-tree; assert distribution is approximately geometric with ratio r (chi-squared or simple ratio check tolerant to ~30%) and that each inserted node has a parent pointer at level+1

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the autoregressive generation loop terminates when an EOS token is produced (Algorithm 1 lines 39-41)

verification.spec: Run generation on a short prompt with max_new_tokens=512; force the model (e.g., via a trivial prompt that produces a short answer) to emit EOS before reaching max tokens; assert that the generation loop exits with the EOS token included as the last generated token and that fewer than max_new_tokens are produced

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that during prefill, GPU forward computation of layer i+1 is overlapped with PCIe KV offload of layer i and CPU-side DCI indexing of layer i, yielding measurable wall-clock speedup over a serial baseline

verification.spec: Run prefill on a 16k-token input through Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with (a) pipelined IceCache and (b) a serial mode that forces sequential prefill->offload->index per layer; measure end-to-end prefill latency for both; assert pipelined latency is at least 1.2x faster than serial

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that CPU-side DCI indexing of layer i runs concurrently with GPU prefill of layer i+1, as described in Figure 5(b).

verification.spec: Instrument the prefill pipeline to record start/end timestamps for (GPU prefill, PCIe offload, CPU DCI indexing) per layer. Run a multi-layer prefill on a synthetic 8k prompt. Assert that for some layer i, the CPU indexing interval of layer i overlaps (in wall-clock time) with the GPU prefill interval of layer i+1 by at least 30% of the shorter of the two intervals.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the IceCache pipelined prefill achieves measurable speedup over a serial workflow (Prefill -> Offload -> Index sequentially per layer).

verification.spec: Implement both a serial workflow (Prefill_Li -> Offload_Li -> Index_Li -> Prefill_{Li+1} ...) and the IceCache pipelined workflow. Run prefill on a 16k-token prompt with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct on an H100. Measure end-to-end prefill latency in both modes. Compute speedup = serial_time / pipelined_time and assert speedup >= 1.2 (paper claims latency is largely hidden).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that selected pages are aggregated into a contiguous CPU pre-allocated buffer prior to PCIe transfer.

verification.spec: Inspect the bulk-loading function. Assert that a pinned CPU buffer is pre-allocated at init (not per-call). Provide a set of K non-contiguous page indices; call the aggregator; assert the resulting CPU buffer is contiguous (single underlying storage, stride matches page_size) and contains the K pages packed consecutively in the order selected.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the CPU->GPU transfer of selected pages occurs via a single high-throughput PCIe transaction (one cudaMemcpyAsync / equivalent) into a pre-allocated GPU buffer.

verification.spec: Monkey-patch / count torch.Tensor.copy_ and cuda memcpy invocations during one backload of K=32 selected pages. Assert exactly one host-to-device memcpy call is issued for the page payload (not K). Also assert the GPU destination buffer was allocated once at init and reused.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that after the bulk PCIe transfer, a scatter operation copies pages from the GPU backload buffer into their exact slots in the KV-cache table.

verification.spec: Construct a KV-cache table with known sentinel values. Stage K pages with distinct content in the GPU backload buffer and call the scatter routine with a mapping {buffer_slot -> kv_table_page_id}. Assert that, after scatter, kv_table[page_id] equals the staged content for each mapped id and other slots are unchanged.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the M-DCI QUERY routine traverses the DCI-tree top-down from level L (root) down to the target level l, as in Algorithm 4.

verification.spec: Build a small DCI-tree with 3 levels and instrument QUERY to log the level index at each iteration. Call QUERY(q, T, l=1, k=5) and assert the logged sequence of visited levels is exactly [L, L-1, ..., 1]. Also assert that at each level the candidate set S' is computed by Prioritized-DCI-Query applied to the candidate nodes from the previous level.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that within each level of M-DCI QUERY, Prioritized-DCI-Query is invoked on each candidate node s in the current set S, and the results are unioned into S'.

verification.spec: Mock Prioritized-DCI-Query to return a deterministic, node-dependent set. Build a DCI-tree with 2 candidate nodes at level i. Call QUERY and assert Prioritized-DCI-Query was invoked once per candidate node and that S' equals the union of the mocked returns.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that QUERY maintains a bounded-size priority queue P (capacity k) into which candidates are inserted whenever Flag=True or i==l, accumulating top-k across levels.

verification.spec: Initialize a 3-level DCI-tree and call QUERY(q, T, l=-1, k=4) (Flag=True). Inject 10 candidates per level with known scores (via mocked Prioritized-DCI-Query). Assert P's final contents are the 4 candidates with the highest inner-product-with-q across all levels and that |P|<=k throughout execution.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that QUERY returns the k nodes from priority queue P ranked by maximum inner-product of their stored keys with the query q_i.

verification.spec: Construct a DCI-tree where each node's keys have known inner products with a fixed query q. Call QUERY(q, T, l=1, k=3). Assert the returned list has length 3 and matches the 3 nodes whose best key has the largest <key, q> value, ordered by descending inner product.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)
Full KV-cache baseline on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct achieves average LongBench accuracy of 49.5 (matching Table 1 FULL row)

verification.spec: Run Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with full (unmodified) KV cache on the 16 LongBench subtasks; compute the average score across tasks; assert measured average is within tolerance of 49.5

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that random projection vectors are sampled and used to compute per-index lower bounds on distances between data points and a query (as in DCI/P-DCI)

verification.spec: Import the indexing module; verify it samples random unit vectors u_j in R^d (one per index), and for arbitrary key k and query q the projected lower bound |<u_j, k - q>| is computed and is always <= ||k - q||_2 (Cauchy-Schwarz). Test on 100 random (k,q) pairs in d=64 and assert the inequality holds in every case.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that a priority queue orders candidate points by their aggregated lower bound distance across multiple DCI indices (Prioritized DCI ordering)

verification.spec: Construct a P-DCI index with m>=4 indices over 500 random vectors in R^32. Issue a query and verify (a) the search pops points in non-decreasing order of aggregated lower-bound score, and (b) the returned top-k matches exact k-NN (k=5) ranking with recall >= 0.9.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that multi-level (M-DCI) recursive search descends the DCI-tree hierarchy: P-DCI at top level finds candidate parents, then search recurses within children at next lower level until level 1 is reached

verification.spec: Build an M-DCI tree on 2000 random d=64 vectors with promotion ratio r=0.1, verify L >= 2 levels exist. Issue 50 queries; assert that the recursive search descends levels top-down (only visits children of selected parents) and returns top-10 nearest neighbours with recall >= 0.8 against brute-force ground truth.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache applied to Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct achieves 100% passkey retrieval accuracy across context lengths of 40k, 50k, 60k, and 70k words with a KV-cache budget of 256 tokens, evaluated on 100 test cases per length with passkey insertion positions ranging from 0% to 95% in 5% increments.

verification.spec: Run the passkey retrieval evaluation harness on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with IceCache (budget=256) for context lengths {40000, 50000, 60000, 70000} words; for each length generate 100 cases with passkey inserted at 20 evenly-spaced positions (0%, 5%, ..., 95%); compute retrieval accuracy per length; assert mean accuracy across the 4 lengths is within tolerance of 1.0.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache applied to Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct achieves 100% passkey retrieval accuracy across context lengths of 80k, 90k, and 100k words with a KV-cache budget of 256 tokens, evaluated on 100 test cases per length with passkey insertion positions ranging from 0% to 95% in 5% increments.

sub_tasks.requirements: Hardware / library platforms used in the paper
Code has been implemented and run such that the system is configured to use 64 CPU threads for DCI-tree CPU-side indexing, matching the paper's experimental setup

verification.spec: Import the IceCache config/runtime module; assert that the configured CPU thread count (e.g., via torch.get_num_threads() or a documented config field like cfg.num_cpu_threads) equals 64; assert that DCI-tree indexing functions are dispatched on CPU (tensors/arrays involved have device='cpu')
verification.paper_cite: Section 5.1: 'we fix the number of CPU threads to 64'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that CPU-GPU transfers are performed over PCIe (consistent with the paper using A100 40GB PCIe / H100 80GB PCIe GPUs, not SXM/NVLink)

verification.spec: Query nvidia-smi or torch.cuda to confirm the GPU model contains 'PCIe' (or the documented hardware is PCIe variant); assert that the bulk-loading code path uses standard cudaMemcpyAsync over PCIe (no NVLink-specific APIs); check that the IceCache transfer buffers are pinned host memory (torch.cuda.is_pinned()) to enable high-throughput PCIe transfers
verification.paper_cite: Section 5.1: 'NVIDIA A100 40GB PCIe GPU ... or an NVIDIA H100 80GB PCIe GPU'

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented such that Figure 2 illustration is reproduced: during prefill, tokens are indexed into a DCI-tree by semantic similarity in the transformed key-embedding space; during decoding, top-k relevant pages are retrieved via tree search; semantically related tokens cluster within the same leaves/pages.

verification.spec: Build DCI-tree on a synthetic key-embedding set of 4096 tokens with planted clusters; query with a vector close to one cluster; assert that retrieved top-k pages contain >=80% of the planted-cluster members (semantic locality check).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the attention weight formula in Equation 1 (softmax of QK^T/sqrt(d) with mask S applied) is correctly computed for the sparse attention path.

verification.spec: Compute attention with full mask vs IceCache sparse mask on a small (n=16, m=64, d=64) random Q,K,V; verify that for selected indices the attention weights match standard softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d)) restricted to those indices within 1e-5.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the DCI-tree mapping between logical nodes and physical KV pages (as illustrated in Figure 3) is functional: each tree node maps to a physical page id with a fill-count, and lookup of selected nodes returns the correct KV embeddings.

verification.spec: Inspect the implemented DCI-tree data structure: assert that it exposes (node_id -> page_id, fill_count, key_ids) mapping; build on 14 tokens with page size 3 (matching Fig 3's 8 nodes example) and verify the mapping is consistent (every key_id appears in exactly one node's page).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the key/query transformation T_K and T_Q (Equation in Section 4.2, mapping R^d -> R^{d+1} with norm-padding using c >= max ||k_j||_2) is implemented correctly so that Euclidean distance between T_K(k) and T_Q(q) is monotonically related to -<q,k>.

verification.spec: Sample random k_1..k_n and q in R^64; compute T_K(k_i), T_Q(q); verify Spearman rank correlation between ||T_Q(q)-T_K(k_i)||_2 and -<q,k_i> across i is >= 0.99.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that bulk loading (Figure 4) aggregates selected non-contiguous KV pages into a contiguous CPU buffer, performs a single PCIe transfer to a GPU buffer, then scatters into KV-cache slots — and is faster than per-page individual transfers.

verification.spec: Transfer 256 KV pages of shape (page_size=16, d=128) from CPU to GPU using (a) per-page cudaMemcpy and (b) bulk-loading buffer; assert bulk-loading is at least 2x faster.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that random level promotion in DCI-tree construction follows the geometric distribution P(level=l) = r^(l-1) - r^l for l < L and r^(L-1) for l = L

verification.spec: Construct a DCI-tree with N=100000 random key embeddings and promotion ratio r=0.1; collect empirical fraction of points at each level; compute the fraction promoted to level >=2 and assert it is within tolerance of r=0.1; also assert empirical level-1 fraction is within tolerance of (1-r)=0.9
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.2: 'the distribution over level indices follows a geometric distribution'

sub_tasks.paper_unit: fraction of points promoted to level >= 2 with r=0.1

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that each point at level l is assigned a parent at level l+1 equal to its nearest neighbor (in transformed key-embedding space) among the promoted points at level l+1, via M-DCI 1-NN query

verification.spec: Build a small DCI-tree with N=1000 synthetic key embeddings (d=64) and r=0.2; for a random sample of 100 lower-level points, look up their assigned parents at the next higher level; compare against brute-force 1-NN over the points actually present at that higher level; assert >=95% match rate (M-DCI is approximate; allow small fraction of mismatches due to randomized index)
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.2: 'each data point at level l is linked to a parent at level l+1, defined as the closest point ... efficiently solved using M-DCI query'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that when a new decoded token's KV is produced, its key embedding is inserted into the DCI-tree and its KV is written into the physical memory page that corresponds to its assigned tree node

verification.spec: Run a short generation (e.g., 32 decode steps) on a tiny model; after each step, inspect the DCI-tree page-table: assert (a) the new token's key ID appears in exactly one leaf node, (b) the corresponding physical KV page on CPU contains the freshly inserted K and V tensors at the expected slot, (c) the page's fill-count is incremented by 1
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.2: 'the key is added to the physical memory page corresponding to the node into which it is inserted'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the first two layers of the model are excluded from IceCache management (no DCI-tree built; KV remains dense on GPU for layers 0 and 1)

verification.spec: Instantiate the IceCache-wrapped model; assert that layers 0 and 1 use the standard dense attention path (no DCI-tree object attached, no CPU offload), while layers >=2 have DCI-tree state; confirm via a forward pass that no CPU<->GPU KV transfer is triggered for layers 0-1
verification.paper_cite: Section 5.1: 'neither IceCache nor baseline methods are applied to the first two layers of the models'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that for layers >=2, the 'middle' KV tensors (excluding sink and window tokens) are copied to CPU pinned memory after prefill

verification.spec: Run prefill on a sequence of length 4096 with a small model; after prefill, for each layer >=2 inspect the IceCache state and assert: the middle-token K and V tensors live on device='cpu', are stored in pinned memory (.is_pinned() == True), and their token count equals seq_len - num_sink - num_window
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.1 / Appendix A: middle tokens are offloaded to CPU; sink and window pages stay on GPU

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that during prefill, a separate DCI-tree is constructed for each attention head (per layer >=2), covering all middle tokens

verification.spec: After prefill on a sequence of length 4096, enumerate the IceCache state for one managed layer; assert the number of DCI-tree objects equals the number of KV heads for that layer; assert each tree indexes exactly the middle-token count; assert trees for different heads are distinct objects with different root-node contents (key embeddings differ across heads)
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.3: 'IceCache constructs a separate DCI-tree for each attention head'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that QUERY(q, T, l=-1, k) returns the top-k approximate nearest keys to q using prioritized DCI traversal recursively at each level of the tree

verification.spec: Build a DCI-tree on N=2000 random key embeddings (d=128); for 50 random queries, run QUERY(q, T, -1, k=20) and compare against brute-force top-20 nearest neighbors (Euclidean on transformed embeddings); assert recall@20 >= 0.7 averaged across queries (M-DCI is approximate)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that FIND-PAGE-INDEX maps a set of key IDs to the unique page IDs that contain them (deduplicating pages)

verification.spec: Construct a small DCI-tree where the key->page mapping is known (e.g., manually assign 100 keys to 10 pages); call FIND-PAGE-INDEX on a list of 30 keys; assert the returned page IDs are (a) the unique set of pages containing those keys, (b) contain no duplicates, (c) every input key's page is included

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that a custom IceCache attention module replaces the HuggingFace default attention implementation for layers >=2 of the wrapped model

verification.spec: Load the wrapped model (e.g., a small Llama model); for layers 0-1 assert the attention module type is the standard HF attention class; for layers >=2 assert the attention module is the IceCache custom class (type name contains 'IceCache' or similar); run a forward pass and confirm the custom path is exercised (e.g., via a counter or hook)
verification.paper_cite: Section 5.1: 'We implement IceCache on top of HuggingFace Transformers, utilizing FlashInfer for the attention kernel'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the IceCache KV-cache class implements the HuggingFace Cache interface and its update() method handles CPU offloading and DCI-tree updates

verification.spec: Import the IceCache cache class; assert it subclasses transformers.cache_utils.Cache (or implements the required methods: update, get_seq_length, reorder_cache); call update() with a small synthetic (key, value) pair for a managed layer and assert (a) the cache's stored token count increases by 1, (b) for sequences exceeding the GPU window, middle KVs are migrated to CPU pinned memory, (c) the DCI-tree for that layer/head reflects the inserted token

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that during generation, an IceCache hook invokes page selection before each decode forward pass and provides the selected pages to the attention computation

verification.spec: Patch the IceCache PAGE-SELECT function with a counter wrapper; run model.generate() for 16 decode steps on a 2048-token prompt with a small wrapped model; assert PAGE-SELECT is called (num_decode_steps * num_managed_layers * num_kv_heads) times (or per-layer, depending on implementation granularity), confirming the selection hook fires before each decode forward pass
verification.paper_cite: Section 4 step (2) page selection happens during token generation

verification.spec: Implement both serial and pipelined prefill orchestrators. Measure end-to-end prefill latency on a fixed-length prompt (e.g., 16k tokens) and assert pipelined latency < serial latency by at least 10%.

verification.spec: Check `/proc/cpuinfo` flags (or equivalent) for 'avx512' presence. The benchmarking harness must gate MagicPig execution on this flag and produce an explicit error or skip otherwise.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 is loaded and evaluated on a single H100 GPU with 64 CPU threads configured for the IceCache CPU-side workload.

verification.spec: Load Qwen/Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 from HuggingFace; confirm torch.cuda.device_count() == 1 and the device is an H100; confirm the configuration sets CPU thread count to 64 (e.g., torch.set_num_threads(64) or env var) and a short generation runs successfully.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented such that the M-DCI multi-level data structure assigns each point a level via the geometric random-promotion process with ratio r, and the empirical level distribution matches the geometric distribution.

verification.spec: Construct a DCI-tree on N=10000 random key embeddings with promotion ratio r=0.5. Assert the fraction of points at level >= L is within 20% of r^(L-1) for L in {1,2,3,4}.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that T_K(k) and T_Q(q) embedding transformations map R^d -> R^(d+1) using a constant c >= max_j ||k_j||, satisfying the property that maximum inner product over keys corresponds to minimum Euclidean distance over transformed vectors.

verification.spec: Generate random keys and queries in R^d. Apply T_K and T_Q. Assert that for each query, argmax_j <q, k_j> equals argmin_j ||T_Q(q) - T_K(k_j)||_2. Also assert c >= max_j ||k_j||.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that Prioritized DCI (P-DCI) querying uses multiple random projection indices and a priority queue ordered by aggregated lower bounds on projected distances.

verification.spec: On a synthetic dataset of 5000 d=64 points, run P-DCI query for k=10 nearest neighbours and compare against brute-force Euclidean k-NN. Assert recall@10 >= 0.8, and verify the implementation uses a priority queue (introspect class/code) and >1 random projection.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that each non-top-level point is assigned a parent at the next higher level, defined as the 1-nearest neighbour among the promoted points, computed via an M-DCI query.

verification.spec: After indexing N=2000 points with promotion ratio r=0.5, for a random sample of 50 non-top-level points, verify the recorded parent is among the top-3 nearest promoted points (allowing ANN approximation) and that the parent lies at level = child_level + 1.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the DCI-tree query and insertion routines use Euclidean distance (||a-b||_2) as the distance function over the transformed embedding space.

verification.spec: Inspect the DCI-tree distance function via importlib; assert it computes Euclidean (L2) distance. Additionally run a unit test where dist(x, y) == np.linalg.norm(x - y, 2) for 50 random pairs.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that each leaf node of the DCI-tree maps one-to-one to a single physical KV memory page.

verification.spec: After indexing, iterate over all leaf nodes and assert there is a bijection between leaf-node ids and physical page ids (no leaf maps to multiple pages and no page belongs to multiple leaves).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the page-mapping table records, for each node, the (page_id, fill_count, list_of_key_ids) tuple matching the structure illustrated in Figure 3.

verification.spec: Construct a small DCI-tree on N=64 keys with page size 4. Inspect the mapping table and assert each entry contains fields {page_id:int, fill_count:int, key_ids:list[int]} and that fill_count == len(key_ids) for every entry.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that tokens sharing the same parent node in the DCI-tree are stored contiguously within the same physical KV page (semantic locality preserved).

verification.spec: Build a DCI-tree on N=512 keys. For each leaf node, retrieve the physical memory addresses (or page indices + offsets) of all its key embeddings and assert they all share the same page id and occupy consecutive slots within that page.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that IceCache(reuse) reuses the same selected KV-page indices across consecutive layers, refreshing the selection only every 3 layers (anchor selection at layers 2, 5, 8, ...).

verification.spec: Run one decoding step with IceCache(reuse) enabled and log the selected page index sets per layer. Assert that the selection is recomputed at layers 2, 5, 8, ... and unchanged for the two intermediate layers in between.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache with the critical-page-reuse variant achieves a time-per-output-token (TPOT) of approximately 0.06 seconds on an A100 40GB GPU using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with a 36k-token input sequence.

verification.spec: Load Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with IceCache(reuse) enabled (page indices reused across layers). Construct a 36k-token input prompt. Generate at least 64 output tokens and compute mean decoding time per output token (excluding first token). Assert |measured_tpot - 0.06| / 0.06 <= 0.30

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the per-subtask LongBench accuracy for IceCache at budgets {64, 128, 256} on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct and Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 matches the paper's Table 1 values within tolerance for each of the 16 individual subtasks (NrtvQA, Qasper, MF-en, HotpotQA, 2WikiMQA, Musique, GovReport, QMSum, MultiNews, TREC, TriviaQA, SAMSum, PCount, PRe, Lcc, RB-P).

verification.spec: For each (model in {Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2}, budget in {64,128,256}, subtask in 16 LongBench subtasks), run IceCache inference on the LongBench dataset for that subtask and compute the subtask metric per LongBench's official scorer. Assert |measured - paper_value_for_cell| <= absolute_tolerance (3.0 points) for each cell. Aggregate pass/fail count, requiring at least 90% of cells to pass.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: average LongBench accuracy (representative cell: Llama-3.1-8B, budget=128)

verification.spec: Group the 16 LongBench subtasks into 6 categories per Table 1 header. For IceCache on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with budget=256, compute the unweighted mean of subtask scores within each category. Assert |measured_category_avg - paper_category_avg| <= 2.0 absolute points for each of the 6 categories (Single-Doc QA ~43.9, Multi-Doc QA ~43.9, Summarization ~28.6, Few-shot ~69.5, Synthetic ~53.4, Code ~59.5).

verification.spec: After computing per-subtask scores for IceCache, compute the unweighted mean across all 16 LongBench subtasks. Verify the resulting averages match: Llama-3.1-8B at budgets 64/128/256 -> 47.8/48.6/49.0; Mistral-7B at budgets 64/128/256 -> 39.0/40.4/41.7. Assert |measured_avg - paper_avg| <= 1.5 absolute points for each of the 6 combinations.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the M-DCI multi-level hierarchical indexing structure assigns each key embedding a level via random promotion with ratio r (geometric distribution), producing a level distribution where the empirical promotion-rate ratio between consecutive levels is approximately r

verification.spec: Build M-DCI index over N=10000 random key embeddings with promotion ratio r=0.5; assert level counts approximately follow geometric distribution: count(level=l+1) / count(level=l) is within [0.4, 0.6] for the populated lower levels, and total levels L satisfies L >= 3

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that P-DCI performs prioritized k-NN search within a single level using a priority queue ordered by the aggregated lower bound from multiple random projection indices

verification.spec: Construct P-DCI index over N=5000 random d=64 vectors with m=5 simple indices and m_c=2 composite indices; for 20 random queries, assert recall@10 (overlap between P-DCI top-10 and brute-force top-10) is >= 0.7 on average

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the key/query transformations T_K and T_Q map d-dimensional embeddings to (d+1)-dimensional vectors where Euclidean distance in the transformed space correctly ranks inner products in the original space

verification.spec: Implement T_K(k) = [k; sqrt(c^2 - ||k||^2)] and T_Q(q) = [q; 0] with c >= max ||k_j||. For 100 random query q and 1000 random keys, assert that argmax_j <q, k_j> equals argmin_j ||T_Q(q) - T_K(k_j)||_2 for at least 95 of 100 queries

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the DCI-tree construction (Algorithm 2) builds a hierarchical index by random promotion of points and assigns each non-top-level point a parent at the next higher level via 1-NN search

verification.spec: Build DCI-tree over N=2000 random d=32 key embeddings with promotion ratio r=0.5; assert (1) every non-root point has a parent at a strictly higher level, (2) the root level is non-empty, (3) for 50 random sampled points, the assigned parent is among the top-3 nearest promoted points at the parent's level by Euclidean distance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the hierarchical k-NN Query (Algorithm 4) descends from the top level L down to the target level, expanding children of selected nodes via Prioritized-DCI-Query at each level

verification.spec: Build a DCI-tree over N=3000 random d=64 vectors; for 30 random queries, run hierarchical QUERY with k=20 to retrieve k-NN at bottom level; assert mean recall@20 against brute-force Euclidean k-NN is >= 0.6

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that selected KV-pages are aggregated into a contiguous CPU buffer, transferred to GPU via a single bulk PCIe copy, and scattered to their final KV-cache slots (rather than per-page small transfers)

verification.spec: Simulate selection of 64 non-contiguous CPU pages each of fixed size; assert the bulk-loading implementation issues exactly one cudaMemcpyAsync (or torch contiguous-to-CUDA copy) for the aggregated buffer (mock/patch torch.Tensor.to or cudaMemcpy and count calls), then performs a scatter into the KV-cache; finally verify the resulting GPU KV-cache contents equal the CPU page contents at the scattered slots

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that prefill, KV-offloading via PCIe, and CPU DCI-tree indexing are pipelined across consecutive layers so that indexing of layer L_i overlaps with prefill/offload of layer L_{i+1}

verification.spec: Run IceCache prefilling on a 16k-token input with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct on H100 with pipelining enabled vs disabled (serial); assert pipelined wall-clock time is at least 20% lower than serial time, demonstrating overlap of GPU prefill, PCIe offload, and CPU indexing

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that during decoding, each newly generated token's key embedding is incrementally inserted into the DCI-tree: assigned a level via random promotion, given a parent at the next level via M-DCI 1-NN query, and added to the corresponding physical memory page

verification.spec: Build initial DCI-tree over 1000 keys; incrementally insert 200 new keys one at a time using the decoding-time insert routine; after insertion assert (1) tree contains 1200 keys, (2) each newly inserted key has a parent at a strictly higher level (or is at root), (3) querying the final tree for the 200 inserted keys returns each key itself in top-5 with recall >= 0.95
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.2, last paragraph on decoding-stage insertion

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the IceCache(reuse) variant reuses the same selected KV-page indices across all decoder layers within a token step (computing the selection once and applying to subsequent layers), reducing per-token decoding latency relative to vanilla IceCache

verification.spec: Run IceCache(reuse) and vanilla IceCache on a 36k-token sequence with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct on A100/H100 with budget=256; assert (1) IceCache(reuse) computes page selection only once (or only in early layers) and reuses indices in later layers (verified via instrumentation/counter), (2) per-token decoding latency of IceCache(reuse) is at most 0.07s vs vanilla IceCache ~0.11s, matching paper Fig 7b within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation studies from the paper

IceCache(reuse) ablation variant is implemented, in which the selected KV-page indices from one decoder layer are reused for subsequent layers (analogous to OmniKV), and it achieves the reported TPOT of ~0.06s/token at 36k on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct

verification.spec: Enable IceCache(reuse) flag; run on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct at 36k input; measure per-token decode latency; assert |measured - 0.06| / 0.06 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the first two layers of the model are excluded from IceCache (and baseline) sparse-attention treatment, consistent with the convention from Tang et al. (2024), and this configuration is exposed/configurable

verification.spec: Inspect IceCache configuration / layer-wrapping code; assert that layers 0 and 1 perform full attention while layers 2..L apply IceCache sparse retrieval

sub_tasks.requirements: Sink pages (initial tokens) and window pages (most recent tokens) are always retained on GPU and excluded from offloading/eviction in the IceCache implementation

verification.spec: Run IceCache on a short input; inspect the KV-cache page table; assert that pages corresponding to sink tokens (start) and the recent-window tokens are present in GPU memory and never offloaded

sub_tasks.requirements: The promotion ratio r of the multi-level DCI-tree is configurable and an ablation/study can be run varying r and measuring its effect on retrieval recall or latency

verification.spec: Construct DCI-trees with r in {0.1, 0.3, 0.5}; verify the resulting tree level counts follow approximately the geometric distribution n_l = r * n_{l-1}; verify retrieval still produces correct top-k

sub_tasks.requirements: A separate DCI-tree is constructed per attention head (head-specific indexing), and an ablation comparing this to a single shared DCI-tree across heads can be performed

verification.spec: Inspect the IceCache code/data structures; assert there is one DCI-tree per (layer, KV-head) pair; assert page selection is performed independently per head (or per GQA group)

sub_tasks.requirements: The PagedAttention page size (number of tokens per page) and token budget are configurable in the IceCache implementation, enabling ablation over different page sizes/budgets

verification.spec: Inspect configuration; vary page_size in {8, 16, 32} and budget in {64, 128, 256}; assert IceCache runs end-to-end and budget*page_size matches retrieved token count

sub_tasks.requirements: Hardware / library platforms used in the paper

The RULER long-context experimental environment is set up: Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 running on a single NVIDIA H100 80GB GPU with 64 CPU threads, CUDA 12.2, PyTorch 2.4.1, HF Transformers 4.57.1, and FlashInfer attention kernel

verification.spec: Load Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 on H100; verify torch.cuda.get_device_name contains 'H100'; verify os.cpu_count or threading config supports 64 threads; verify flashinfer is importable; run a short generation

sub_tasks.requirements: The implementation pins FlashInfer to version compatible with the paper's stack (PyTorch 2.4.1, CUDA 12.2, Transformers 4.57.1); a requirements file or environment spec lists flashinfer as a dependency and the attention kernel calls route through FlashInfer.

verification.spec: Check that requirements.txt (or pyproject) pins flashinfer; importlib.import_module('flashinfer'); assert torch.__version__.startswith('2.4') and transformers.__version__ == '4.57.1'

sub_tasks.requirements: The latency experiments document the PCIe variant of the A100 40GB (not SXM) in the experimental setup, since CPU-GPU offloading bandwidth assumptions depend on PCIe Gen4 ~32 GB/s.

verification.spec: Grep README/config for 'A100 40GB PCIe' string and confirm the latency benchmark script records the GPU name via torch.cuda.get_device_name(0); assert 'PCIe' appears in documentation.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented and run such that the DCI-tree indexing algorithm (Algorithm 2) is implemented and produces a multi-level hierarchical index where the number of points at each level decays geometrically with promotion ratio r

verification.spec: Implement DCI-INDEXING(S_k, r) as in Algorithm 2; on a synthetic set of 10000 random d=128 key vectors with r=0.25, build the index and verify that for each level l, |n_l - r * n_{l-1}| / n_{l-1} <= 0.20, and that the QUERY function returns valid parent assignments

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the bulk loading mechanism aggregates selected non-contiguous KV-pages into a contiguous CPU buffer and performs a single PCIe transfer to a GPU buffer, then scatters to the KV-Cache table

verification.spec: Implement the bulk back-loading workflow described in Figure 4 and Section 4.5; verify that for a set of N non-contiguous CPU-resident pages, exactly one cudaMemcpyAsync (or equivalent) is invoked per call, and that the resulting GPU KV-cache entries are bitwise-identical to a naive per-page copy reference

sub_tasks.paper_unit: number of PCIe transactions per bulk load

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the prefill pipelining (Figure 5b) overlaps GPU prefill of layer L_{i+1} with PCIe offload of L_i and CPU-side DCI indexing of L_i, yielding lower end-to-end prefill latency than the serial baseline (Figure 5a)

verification.spec: Measure end-to-end prefill latency on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct at 36k context for two configurations: (a) serial prefill->offload->index and (b) IceCache pipelined version; assert pipelined latency is at least 20% lower than serial

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to generate RULER Single-NIAH, Multi-keys NIAH, and QA inputs at 150k, 200k, and 250k token lengths using the Qwen3 tokenizer, producing exactly the prompt lengths required for the RULER evaluation in Table 4.

verification.spec: Call generate_ruler_inputs(task='single_niah', length=150000, tokenizer='Qwen/Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507'); assert max(token_count) is within 5% of 150000 and that Multi-keys and QA variants are also generated for 150k/200k/250k.

sub_tasks.requirements: Key/query transformations T_K and T_Q mapping R^d -> R^(d+1) are implemented such that T_K(k)=[k; sqrt(c^2-||k||^2)] and T_Q(q)=[q; 0] (or analogous reduction of MIPS to Euclidean NN), with c >= max_j ||k_j||. The implementation preserves the property that argmax inner_product(q,k) = argmin Euclidean(T_Q(q), T_K(k)).

verification.spec: Sample 100 random keys k_i in R^128 and a query q; compute c = max ||k_i||; apply T_K, T_Q; assert that the index of argmin ||T_Q(q)-T_K(k_i)||_2 equals argmax <q, k_i>.

sub_tasks.requirements: Prioritized DCI (P-DCI) k-NN query routine (used inside Algorithm 4) is implemented: it builds multiple simple indices via random projections, uses a priority queue ordered by aggregated lower-bound distance, and returns the k approximate nearest neighbors with significantly fewer distance evaluations than brute force.

verification.spec: Build P-DCI over 10k random R^64 points; query 50 random points with k=10; assert recall@10 vs brute-force >= 0.8 and the number of distance computations is < 30% of N.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that a Multi-level DCI (M-DCI) tree can be constructed from a set of key embeddings using random promotion with ratio r (Algorithm 2), producing a hierarchical structure where each point at level l has a parent at level l+1 determined by 1-nearest-neighbor search, and the level distribution approximately follows a geometric distribution with parameter r.

verification.spec: Import the DCI-tree indexing function (e.g., dci_indexing(keys, r)). Construct an index on n=2000 random d=64 key vectors with r=0.5. Assert: (1) tree has L>=2 levels, (2) fraction of points promoted to level >=2 is within [0.35, 0.65] of expected r, (3) every non-root node has exactly one parent at a higher level, (4) for a sample of points, the assigned parent is among the closest promoted points by Euclidean distance on transformed embeddings.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that newly generated tokens during decoding are inserted into the DCI-tree by (a) assigning a level via the same random promotion process with ratio r, (b) finding the parent at the next higher level via M-DCI query, and (c) adding the key to the physical memory page corresponding to that node, allocating a new page if the node exceeds the max page size.

verification.spec: Build an initial DCI-tree over 500 random key embeddings, then incrementally insert 200 new keys one by one. Assert: (1) total point count equals 700, (2) each inserted key is present in exactly one leaf page, (3) when a leaf node fills beyond max_page_size, a new page is allocated and tree integrity (parent pointers, level invariants) is preserved, (4) querying for an inserted key returns it among top-k with k small.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that page selection retrieves top-k relevant pages per attention head via M-DCI query (Algorithm 3), and for group-query attention (GQA) the selected pages across query heads within the same KV group are unioned and shared.

verification.spec: Construct per-head DCI-trees for a small synthetic GQA configuration (e.g., 8 query heads, 2 KV heads -> 4 queries per group). Run page_select(q_per_head, trees, k) and assert: (1) each KV-head returns at most k pages, (2) the returned page set for a group equals the union of selections from its query heads, (3) for a planted query whose nearest keys live in a known page, that page is included in top-k selection with recall >= 0.9 over 50 random trials.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that bulk loading aggregates selected (non-contiguous) KV pages into a contiguous CPU staging buffer, performs a single PCIe transfer to a pre-allocated GPU buffer, and then scatters the pages into their correct slots in the GPU KV-cache table; pages already resident on the GPU from previous steps are filtered out before transfer.

verification.spec: Simulate a CPU-resident KV-cache pool and a GPU KV-cache table. Mark a subset of pages as already-on-GPU. Call bulk_load(selected_page_ids) and assert: (1) only not-yet-resident pages are copied, (2) exactly one cudaMemcpyAsync / torch.Tensor.to call is issued (mockable / countable), (3) after scatter, the GPU KV-cache table at the target slots bitwise-equals the original CPU pages, (4) throughput on a 100-page transfer beats a naive per-page-copy baseline by >=2x on the same hardware.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that prefill, KV offloading over PCIe, and CPU-side DCI indexing are pipelined across layers (Figure 5b): once layer i's KVs arrive in CPU memory, DCI-tree construction for layer i runs in parallel with GPU prefill and PCIe offload of layer i+1, reducing end-to-end prefill latency compared to the serial workflow.

verification.spec: Run prefill on a 16k-token prompt with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct under (a) serial workflow and (b) IceCache pipelined workflow. Measure wall-clock prefill latency for both. Assert pipelined latency is at least 20% lower than serial. Also verify via instrumentation that for layer i+1's prefill, layer i's DCI indexing thread is active concurrently for >50% of the prefill duration.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that sink pages (containing the first tokens of the prompt) and window pages (containing the most recent tokens) are always retained on the GPU and excluded from offloading/eviction, while only middle tokens participate in DCI-tree-based selection.

verification.spec: Configure IceCache with sink_size=S and window_size=W. After prefill on a long prompt and several decoding steps, assert: (1) the S earliest tokens' pages are flagged as sink and resident on GPU at every step, (2) the W most recent tokens' pages are flagged as window and resident on GPU at every step, (3) sink and window pages are NOT inserted into the DCI-tree used for retrieval, (4) page selection only returns indices from the middle (non-sink, non-window) region.
