sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the StreamingLLM baseline achieves TT2T of approximately 0.13 seconds on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct at 36k input length on A100

verification.spec: Implement/run the StreamingLLM baseline on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct (sink + window, budget=256) with a 36k-token input on A100. Measure TT2T over 3 runs. Assert |measured - 0.13| / 0.13 <= 0.35.

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation studies from the paper

Code has been implemented and run such that IceCache(r) (page-index-reuse variant) with budget=256 on Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 with 150k-token RULER context achieves Single-NIAH=100.0, Multi-keys NIAH=98.0, QA=91.0

verification.spec: Run IceCache(reuse) (page-index reuse across layers, budget=256) with Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 on RULER tasks at 150k context length; measure Single-NIAH, Multi-keys NIAH, and QA accuracies; assert all three within 20% relative tolerance of (100.0, 98.0, 91.0)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the IceCache(reuse) ablation — reusing the same selected KV-page indices across layers — achieves the reported TT2T (5.9 s) and TPOT (0.06 s/token) at 36k sequence length with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct (Figure 7)

verification.spec: Run IceCache(reuse) variant on a 36k-token input with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct on A100; measure TT2T; assert |measured - 5.9| / 5.9 <= 0.25 AND TPOT |measured - 0.06| / 0.06 <= 0.30

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the ablation of applying IceCache to all layers (vs. the default of skipping the first 2 layers) is measured on LongBench with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, showing the accuracy degradation that motivates the first-two-layer skip

verification.spec: Run IceCache with budget=256 on LongBench with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct in two configurations: (a) skipping the first 2 layers (default, expected avg ~49.0) and (b) applying IceCache to ALL layers; verify (a) accuracy >= (b) accuracy, demonstrating the first-two-layer-skip benefit; assert |default_accuracy - 49.0| / 49.0 <= 0.10
verification.paper_cite: Section 5.1: 'neither IceCache nor baseline methods are applied to the first two layers of the models'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the promotion ratio r in M-DCI is ablated across at least three values (e.g., r in {0.25, 0.5, 0.75}) and retrieval recall / LongBench accuracy is reported per setting, demonstrating sensitivity of IceCache to r.

verification.spec: Run a sweep over r in {0.25, 0.5, 0.75} with a fixed cache budget of 256 on a LongBench subset using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct; assert that an ablation table is produced with one row per r and accuracy column populated; assert best-r accuracy is within 5 absolute points of the reported IceCache value (49.0).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that an ablation comparing per-head DCI-tree page selection vs. a shared (single) page-selection scheme across heads is reported, showing per-head selection yields higher accuracy.

verification.spec: Run IceCache with (a) per-head DCI-tree page selection and (b) a shared-pages-across-heads variant on a LongBench subset using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with budget=256; assert the per-head variant's average accuracy exceeds the shared variant by at least 1 absolute point.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that prefill latency with bulk-loading + pipelining is measured against a serial (no-pipeline) baseline on a 36k-token sequence, demonstrating reduced end-to-end prefill latency.

verification.spec: Run prefill on a 36k-token input with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct in two modes (serial vs. pipelined-bulk-load); record wall-clock prefill latency for each; assert pipelined latency is strictly less than serial latency and report the ratio (target speedup ~1.3x).

sub_tasks.requirements: Hardware / library platforms used in the paper
Code has been implemented such that the first 2 layers of the model are excluded from IceCache (i.e., use full attention for layers 0 and 1), matching the paper's implementation detail.

verification.spec: Inspect the IceCache model wrapper configuration; assert the layer-skip parameter equals 2 and that during a forward pass layers 0 and 1 do not invoke the DCI-tree page selection path (e.g., via a hook counting calls).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that for group-query attention (GQA) models, the page-selection results across query heads within the same KV group are aggregated via union of selected pages, and the union is shared across the group.

verification.spec: Construct a toy GQA configuration (n_q_heads=4, n_kv_heads=2); for a synthetic query, run head-level page selection and call the GQA aggregation function; assert the resulting page set for each KV group equals the union of the per-query-head selected pages and is identical for all query heads in the same group.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)
Code has been implemented such that the DCI-tree data structure supports hierarchical levels with random promotion of points (promotion ratio r) following Algorithm 2.

verification.spec: Insert N=10000 random d=64 key embeddings into the DCI-tree with r=0.5; assert the number of points at each level approximately follows a geometric distribution (level_{l+1}/level_l within [0.3, 0.7]); assert every non-top-level point has a parent assigned at the next higher level.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the Prioritized DCI (P-DCI) query routine returns the k nearest neighbors using lower-bound priority ordering (Algorithm 4 inner query).

verification.spec: Construct a single-level P-DCI index over N=1000 random d=64 points; for 20 random queries, compute Prioritized-DCI-Query top-k (k=10) and brute-force exact top-10; assert recall@10 >= 0.8 averaged across queries.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the M-DCI multi-level query recursively descends from the top level, calling P-DCI at each level and restricting the search to children of selected nodes (Section 3.3).

verification.spec: Build a 3-level DCI-tree over 5000 random d=64 points; run M-DCI query for k=20 over 20 random queries; assert (a) recall@20 vs brute-force >= 0.7, and (b) M-DCI distance computations are strictly fewer than brute-force.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that key and query embeddings are transformed via T_K and T_Q with appending a coordinate based on c >= max_j ||k_j||_2, enabling inner-product search via Euclidean distance.

verification.spec: Generate random key matrix K (m=200, d=64) and queries Q (n=20, d=64); compute c = max ||k_j||; apply T_K and T_Q to produce (d+1)-dim vectors; assert that for each query q, argmin_j ||T_Q(q) - T_K(k_j)||_2 equals argmax_j <q, k_j> (exact match for all 20 queries).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that a separate DCI-tree is constructed per attention head, allowing different page selections per head.

verification.spec: Instantiate IceCache for a model with H attention heads; assert the cache exposes a list/dict of H independent DCI-tree objects (one per head); for two heads, query with distinct synthetic keys and assert returned page-id sets can differ.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that for GQA, page indices from all query heads within a KV-group are merged via set-union before loading pages.

verification.spec: Mock 4 query heads sharing 1 KV head with per-head page selections {A,B}, {B,C}, {A,D}, {E}; call the GQA aggregation function and assert the resulting selected-page set equals {A,B,C,D,E}.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that PAGE-SELECT (Algorithm 3) calls QUERY to get top-k keys and uses FIND-PAGE-INDEX to map them to the unique set of page IDs containing those keys.

verification.spec: Build a DCI-tree over 500 random keys with page_size=16; call PAGE-SELECT(q, T, k=32) for a random query; assert (a) the returned page IDs are unique, (b) every returned page id corresponds to at least one of the top-32 retrieved key indices, and (c) the union of keys in returned pages is a superset of the retrieved top-k key set.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that during decoding, each newly generated key embedding is dynamically inserted into the DCI-tree using random level promotion and parent assignment via 1-NN search.

verification.spec: Build a DCI-tree over 1000 initial keys; then insert 200 new keys one at a time using the decode-time insertion routine; assert that after each insertion the tree is well-formed (each non-root node has exactly one parent at the next higher level), and that querying for the inserted key returns it among the top-1 retrieved keys in >= 90% of cases.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the node-to-page mapping enforces a maximum page size, allocating new pages when a node's token count exceeds the limit.

verification.spec: Set max page size P=8 and insert keys so a single node receives 25 tokens; assert that the node maps to ceil(25/8)=4 distinct physical page IDs and that each page holds at most 8 token entries.
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.1 (page split when node exceeds max page size)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that selected non-contiguous KV pages are aggregated into a contiguous CPU buffer, transferred to GPU via a single PCIe transaction, and then scattered into the GPU KV-cache table.

verification.spec: Allocate a mock CPU KV-cache with 64 pages of shape (page_size, d); request a non-contiguous subset of 8 page IDs; call bulk_backload(selected_ids); assert (a) exactly one cuda memcpy host->device call is issued (verified via a torch profiler or a counter), and (b) the GPU KV-cache table contents at the selected positions match the original CPU page contents bitwise.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the IceCache prefill pipeline overlaps GPU prefill computation with PCIe KV-offloading and CPU-side DCI-tree indexing, so that indexing of layer L_i proceeds in parallel with prefilling/offloading of layer L_{i+1}

verification.spec: Instrument the prefill loop to record per-layer timestamps for GPU prefill, PCIe offload, and CPU DCI indexing across N=4 layers. Assert that the CPU indexing interval for layer L_i overlaps (start < end of next stage) with the GPU prefill interval for layer L_{i+1}. Also assert end-to-end prefill latency is strictly less than the serial sum of (prefill + offload + index) times by at least 15%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that tokens are partitioned into sink tokens (initial tokens), window tokens (most recent tokens), and middle tokens, with sink pages and window pages kept resident on GPU while middle-token pages are managed by the DCI-tree and offloaded to CPU

verification.spec: Import the IceCache cache module and instantiate it with a sequence of length 4096, sink_size=4, window_size=128. Assert that get_sink_pages() returns pages covering tokens [0:4], get_window_pages() returns pages covering the last 128 tokens, and these pages are resident on GPU (device.type == 'cuda'). Assert middle-token pages are tracked by the DCI-tree and stored on CPU (device.type == 'cpu').
